pi_items.source: Task description specifies creating a field labeled 'Engraving Message'. Verified against task.json description and export_result.sh which searches for fields containing 'engraving' in the name.

Verified against official Drupal Commerce documentation (docs.drupalcommerce.org/v2/developer-guide/orders/), GitHub drupalcommerce/commerce repository, and export_result.sh which references field.storage.commerce_order_item configuration. This is the standard entity type for line items in orders in Drupal Commerce.

Verified against Drupal Commerce documentation which confirms 'default' is the standard bundle name for order item types. Confirmed in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh which reference 'field.field.commerce_order_item.default.*' configuration paths.

Verified against official Drupal Commerce documentation (docs.drupalcommerce.org/v2/developer-guide/cart/customize-cart/) which documents the 'add_to_cart' form display mode. Confirmed in export_result.sh which references 'core.entity_form_display.commerce_order_item.default.add_to_cart' configuration. This form mode is created by the Commerce Cart module and is the standard way to customize what fields appear on the Add to Cart form.

privileged_info_summary: The task is a Drupal Commerce configuration exercise where an agent must add a custom text field to the commerce_order_item entity's default bundle and configure it to appear on the add_to_cart form display. The entity type commerce_order_item is the standard Drupal Commerce entity for representing line items in orders. The default bundle is the standard order item type. The add_to_cart form mode is a real form display mode in Drupal Commerce created by the Cart module for configuring which fields appear on product add-to-cart forms. The expected field label should contain the keyword 'Engraving' (specifically 'Engraving Message' per task description). All metadata claims are accurate and consistent with Drupal Commerce architecture and the task requirements.
